name: Sofiane Azogagh
title: Postdoctoral Researcher
about: I am a Postdoctoral Researcher at EURECOM, working with [Melek Önen](http://www.eurecom.fr/en/people/onen-melek) on security and privacy in the context of Machine Learning. My current research centers on prompt injection attacks and defenses for LLMs, alongside a range of privacy-enhancing technologies such as Federated Learning, Homomorphic Encryption, and Private Set Intersection.

In 2026, I completed my PhD at Université du Québec à Montréal (UQÀM) under the supervision of [Marc-Olivier Killijian](https://kirija.github.io) and [Sébastien Gambs](https://sebastiengambs.openum.ca/team/). My thesis focused on the design of oblivious algorithms on encrypted data using homomorphic encryption to enhance data privacy in machine learning. I also worked on privacy-preserving graph analysis (PageRank, Common Neighbors, etc.).
